steps.text: Lead the response to the outbreak of Zika.
Issue an advisory to alert US healthcare providers and the public about the threat of Zika.
Encourage US healthcare providers to report suspected Zika cases to public health departments.
Develop communication materials for Olympic athletes.

preconditions.text: Before issuing an advisory, ensure there is evidence of infections being detected in US-bound travelers from countries with Zika.
If there is a suspected link between Zika and microcephaly, advise pregnant women to postpone travel to areas with Zika.

steps.text: Issue travel notices over the course of the outbreak.
Coordinate activities with the US Olympic Committee to help travelers and athletes take steps to protect their health during their trips.
Provide guidance and information for all travelers about the status of Zika in the region.
Travel to Houston, Texas, to meet with athletes before heading to Brazil.
Distribute Zika prevention information to athletes.
Answer athletes' specific questions about staying healthy while in Rio.
Remind athletes of precautions to take after returning home.
Partner with the US Department of State in Brazil to provide Zika information to travelers as they arrive for the Games.
Establish the Antibiotic Resistance Laboratory Network (ARLN).
Enhance local lab capacity and strengthen regional and national coordination.
Conduct nationwide testing to fill data gaps and inform prevention and response for several urgent and serious pathogen threats.
Support other AR priorities.
Improve rapid detection and faster response to outbreaks and emerging resistance.
Expand overseas tuberculosis (TB) screenings and establish a national drug stockpile.
Improve prevention of healthcare-associated infections (HAIs), foodborne illness, and gonorrhea.
Empower networks for preventing AR threats across healthcare and communities.
Provide insights for research innovation and better patient care.
Ensure antibiotics work to protect patients from life-threatening infections or sepsis.
Search for mcr -1 in bacterial samples from human, retail meat, and food animal sources.
Identify the gene in bacterial samples.
Notify states, hospitals, and clinical laboratories about the discovery of mcr -1.
Develop and deploy a rapid laboratory test to help clinical labs find bacteria with the mcr -1 gene.
Test other new diagnostics that can rapidly detect bacteria with the gene.
Confirm that flour was the source of an E. coli outbreak.
Use whole genome sequencing to confirm that flour was the culprit.
Collaborate with partners in Canada to share whole genome sequence data.
Identify packaged lettuce as a new source for listeriosis outbreaks.
Encourage practices to reduce Salmonella in birds.
Increase education for flock owners and industry about staying healthy while keeping backyard flocks.
Alert hospitals and clinicians about the threat of heater-cooler unit contamination, and offer guidance for informing patients at risk.
Make recommendations about how to detect adverse events during dialysis.
